source: Label 309 unknown-envelope rule — role-dependent dual-severity dispositions
description: Each case pins one record body and two expected dispositions for the same bytes: `public` is the default public-verifier reading (the envelope degrades to opaque; ENC_UNSUPPORTED is info; the content-hash claim is still validated), `recipient_or_strict` is the recipient-verifier / strict-sealed-crypto-mode reading (ENC_UNSUPPORTED escalates to error and co-fires with the identifier-specific UNSUPPORTED_* code). Codes are listed sorted; implementations MUST emit exactly these sets at the stated severities.

vectors.name: enc-scheme-2-opaque
vectors.note: An enc.scheme outside the supported set makes the whole envelope opaque in the public reading: the non-scheme-1 keys receive no shape validation, the item is tagged ENC_UNSUPPORTED (info), and the content-hash claim still validates. The recipient role and strict sealed-crypto mode reject instead, co-firing the scheme-specific code.

vectors.name: enc-kem-unregistered-opaque
vectors.note: An unregistered enc.kem makes the envelope opaque in the public reading: no slot-shape or length rule is applied under an unknown KEM (the 16-byte epk and 8-byte wrap here MUST NOT surface KEM_EPK_LENGTH_MISMATCH / WRAP_LENGTH_MISMATCH). The strict reading rejects with the KEM-specific code.

vectors.name: enc-aead-unregistered-opaque
vectors.note: An authenticated but unregistered aead makes the envelope opaque in the public reading — the 12-byte nonce MUST NOT surface NONCE_LENGTH_MISMATCH, because no nonce-length rule exists under an unknown content format. Unauthenticated cipher families are the carve-out: they reject with UNAUTHENTICATED_CIPHER_FORBIDDEN in every role.

vectors.name: enc-scheme-2-content-hash-rule-still-applies
vectors.note: ENC_REQUIRES_CONTENT_HASH inspects the item's hashes map, not the envelope, so it applies even when the envelope is opaque: an enc-bearing item with no registered content-hash key fails in every role.
